1_1_1: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth.
1_1_2: The earth was formless and void, and darkness was over the face of the deep. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the surface of the waters.
1_1_3: Then God said, "Let there be light"; and there was light.
1_1_4: And God saw that the light was good; and God separated the light from the darkness.
1_1_5: God called the light "day," and the darkness he called "night." And there was evening and there was morning, the first day.
1_1_6: Then God said, "Let there be an expanse in the midst of the waters, and let it separate the waters from the waters."
1_1_7: So God made the expanse and separated the waters which were below the expanse from the waters which were above the expanse; and it was so.
1_1_8: And God called the expanse "heaven." And there was evening and there was morning, the second day.
1_1_9: Then God said, "Let the waters under the heavens be gathered together into one place, and let the dry land appear"; and it was so.
1_1_10: And God called the dry land "earth," and the gathering of the waters He called "seas." And God saw that it was good.
1_1_11: Then God said, "Let the earth produce vegetation: seed-bearing plants and fruit trees on the earth bearing fruit with seed in it according to their kinds." And it was so.
1_1_12: The earth brought forth vegetation: plants yielding seed according to their kinds and trees bearing fruit with seed in them according to their kinds. And God saw that it was good.
1_1_13: And there was evening and there was morning, the third day.
1_1_14: Then God said, "Let there be lights in the expanse of the heavens to separate the day from the night. And let them be for signs and for seasons, and for days and years."
1_1_15: and let them be for lights in the expanse of the heavens to give light on the earth"; and it was so.
1_1_16: Then God made two great lights—the greater light to rule the day and the lesser light to rule the night—and the stars also.
1_1_17: God set them in the expanse of the heavens to give light on the earth,
1_1_18: to rule over the day and over the night, and to separate the light from the darkness. And God saw that it was good.
1_1_19: And there was evening and there was morning, the fourth day.
1_1_20: Then God said, "Let the waters swarm with living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the expanse of the heavens."
1_1_21: So God created the great sea creatures and every living creature that moves, with which the waters swarm according to their kinds, and every winged bird according to its kind. And God saw that it was good.
1_1_22: And God blessed them, saying, "Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let birds multiply on the earth."
1_1_23: And there was evening and there was morning, the fifth day.
1_1_24: Then God said, "Let the earth bring forth living creatures according to their kinds: livestock, creeping things, and wild animals of the earth according to their kinds"; and it was so.
1_1_25: And God made the wild animals of the earth according to their kind, the livestock according to their kind, and everything that creeps on the earth according to its kind. And God saw that it was good.
1_1_26: Then God said, "Let us make man in our image, according to our likeness; let them have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the birds of the heavens and over the livestock and over all the earth and over every creeping thing that creeps on the earth."
1_1_27: So God created man in his own image, in the image of God he created him; male and female he created them.
1_1_28: Then God blessed them, and God said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it. Have dominion over the fish of the sea and over the birds of the heavens and over every living thing that moves on the earth."
1_1_29: And God said, "See, I have given you every plant yielding seed that is on the surface of all the earth, and every tree whose fruit yields seed; to you it shall be for food."
1_1_30: And to every beast of the earth and to every bird of the heavens and to everything that creeps on the earth, in which there is life, I have given every green herb for food"; and it was so.
1_1_31: Then God saw everything that he had made, and behold, it was very good. And there was evening and there was morning, the sixth day.
1_2_1: Thus the heavens and the earth were finished, and all the host of them were completed.
1_2_2: By the seventh day God had completed His work which He had done, and He rested on the seventh day from all His work which He had done.
1_2_3: Then God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it, because on it He rested from all His work of creation which God had created and made.
1_2_4: This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created, in the day that the LORD God made the earth and the heavens.
1_2_5: Now no shrub of the field was yet in the earth and no plant of the field had yet sprouted, for the LORD God had not caused it to rain upon the earth, and there was no man to cultivate the ground.
1_2_6: But a mist went up from the earth and watered the whole face of the ground.
1_2_7: Then the LORD God formed the man of dust from the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life, and the man became a living being.
1_2_8: The LORD God planted a garden in Eden, in the east, and there He placed the man whom He had formed.
1_2_9: Out of the ground the LORD God made every tree grow that is pleasant to the sight and good for food, with the tree of life also in the midst of the garden, and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil.
1_2_10: A river flowed out of Eden to water the garden, and from there it divided and became four rivers.
1_2_11: The name of the first is Pishon; it flows around the whole land of Havilah, where there is gold.
1_2_12: The gold of that land is good; bdellium and the onyx stone are there.
1_2_13: The name of the second river is Gihon; it flows around the whole land of Cush.
1_2_14: The name of the third river is Hiddekel, which goes toward the east of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
1_2_15: Then the LORD God took the man and put him into the garden of Eden to cultivate it and keep it.
1_2_16: And the LORD God commanded the man, saying, 'Of every tree of the garden you may freely eat.'
1_2_17: But from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you shall not eat, for in the day that you eat of it you shall surely die.'
1_2_18: Then the LORD God said, 'It is not good that the man should be alone; I will make him a helper suitable and comparable to him.'
1_2_19: Out of the ground the LORD God formed every beast of the field and every bird of the air, and brought them to the man to see what he would call them; and whatever the man called each living creature, that was its name.
1_2_20: The man gave names to all cattle, to the birds of the air, and to every beast of the field, but for the man there was not found a helper comparable to him.
1_2_21: So the LORD God caused a deep sleep to fall upon the man, and he slept; then He took one of his ribs and closed up the flesh at that place.
1_2_22: Then the rib which the LORD God had taken from the man He made into a woman and brought her to the man.
1_2_23: And the man said, 'This at last is bone of my bones and flesh of my flesh; she shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.'
1_2_24: Therefore a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and they shall become one flesh.
1_2_25: And they were both naked, the man and his wife, and were not ashamed.
1_3_1: Now the serpent was more cunning and crafty than any beast of the field which the LORD God had made. He said to the woman, 'Has God indeed said, or did God really say, that you shall not eat of every tree of the garden?'
1_3_2: And the woman said to the serpent, 'We may eat the fruit of the trees of the garden;'
1_3_3: but of the fruit of the tree which is in the midst of the garden, God has said, 'You shall not eat it, nor shall you touch it, lest you die.'"
1_3_4: Then the serpent said to the woman, 'You will not surely die.'
1_3_5: For God knows that in the day you eat of it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil."
1_3_6: So when the woman saw that the tree was good for food, that it was pleasant and a delight to the eyes, and a tree desirable to make one wise, she took of its fruit and ate. She also gave to her husband with her, and he ate.
1_3_7: Then the eyes of both of them were opened, and they knew that they were naked; and they sewed fig leaves together and made themselves coverings.
1_3_8: And they heard the sound of the LORD God walking in the garden in the cool of the day, and Adam and his wife hid themselves from the presence of the LORD God among the trees of the garden.
1_3_9: Then the LORD God called to Adam and said to him, 'Where are you?'
1_3_10: So he said, 'I heard Your voice in the garden, and I was afraid because I was naked; and I hid myself.'
1_3_11: And He said, 'Who told you that you were naked? Have you eaten from the tree of which I commanded you that you should not eat?'
1_3_12: Then the man said, 'The woman whom You gave to be with me, she gave me of the tree, and I ate.'
1_3_13: And the LORD God said to the woman, 'What is this you have done?' The woman said, 'The serpent deceived me, and I ate.'
1_3_14: So the LORD God said to the serpent: 'Because you have done this, you are cursed more than all cattle, and more than every beast of the field; on your belly you shall go, and dust you shall eat all the days of your life.'
1_3_15: And I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your seed and her Seed; He shall bruise your head, and you shall bruise His heel."
1_3_16: To the woman He said: 'I will greatly multiply your sorrow and your pain in childbearing; in pain you shall bring forth children; Your desire shall be for your husband, and he shall rule over you.'
1_3_17: Then to Adam He said, 'Because you have listened to the voice of your wife, and have eaten from the tree of which I commanded you, saying, "You shall not eat of it": Cursed is the ground for your sake; in toil you shall eat of it all the days of your life.'
1_3_18: Both thorns and thistles it shall bring forth for you, and you shall eat the herb of the field.
1_3_19: In the sweat of your face you shall eat bread till you return to the ground, for out of it you were taken; for dust you are, and to dust you shall return."
1_3_20: And Adam called his wife's name Eve, because she was the mother of all living.
1_3_21: Also for Adam and his wife the LORD God made tunics of skin, and clothed them.
1_3_22: Then the LORD God said, 'Behold, the man has become like one of Us, to know good and evil. And now, lest he put out his hand and take also of the tree of life, and eat, and live forever'—
1_3_23: therefore the LORD God sent him out of the garden of Eden to till the ground from which he was taken.
1_3_24: So He drove out the man; and He placed cherubim at the east of the garden of Eden, and a flaming sword which turned every way, to guard the way to the tree of life.
1_4_1: Now Adam knew his wife Eve, and she conceived and bore Cain. She said, "I have acquired a man from the LORD."
1_4_2: Then she bore again his brother Abel. Now Abel was a keeper of sheep, but Cain was a tiller of the ground.
1_4_3: In the course of time Cain brought an offering to the LORD of the fruit of the ground.
1_4_4: Abel also brought of the firstborn of his flock and of their fat portions. And the LORD had regard for Abel and his offering,
1_4_5: but He did not regard Cain and his offering. So Cain became very angry, and his countenance fell.
1_4_6: Then the LORD said to Cain, "Why are you angry? And why has your countenance fallen?
1_4_7: If you do well, will you not be accepted? And if you do not do well, sin is crouching at the door. Its desire is for you, but you must rule over it."
1_4_8: Cain spoke to Abel his brother. And when they were in the field, Cain rose up against Abel his brother and killed him.
1_4_9: Then the LORD said to Cain, "Where is Abel your brother?" He said, "I do not know. Am I my brother's keeper?"
1_4_10: And the LORD said, "What have you done? The voice of your brother's blood cries out to Me from the ground."
1_4_11: Now you are cursed from the earth, which has opened its mouth to receive your brother's blood from your hand.
1_4_12: When you till the ground, it shall no longer yield its strength to you. You will be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth."
1_4_13: And Cain said to the LORD, "My punishment is greater than I can bear!"
1_4_14: Behold, You have driven me out this day from the face of the ground; and from Your face I shall be hidden. I shall be a fugitive and a wanderer on the earth, and whoever finds me will kill me."
1_4_15: Then the LORD said to him, "Therefore, whoever kills Cain, vengeance shall be taken on him sevenfold." And the LORD set a mark on Cain, lest anyone finding him should kill him.
1_4_16: Then Cain went out from the presence of the LORD and settled in the land of Nod, east of Eden.
1_4_17: And Cain knew his wife, and she conceived and bore Enoch. And he built a city, and called the name of the city after the name of his son—Enoch.
1_4_18: To Enoch was born Irad; and Irad became the father of Mehujael, and Mehujael became the father of Methushael, and Methushael became the father of Lamech.
1_4_19: Then Lamech took for himself two wives: the name of one was Adah, and the name of the second was Zillah.
1_4_20: And Adah bore Jabal. He was the father of those who dwell in tents and have livestock.
1_4_21: His brother's name was Jubal; he was the father of all those who play the harp and flute.
1_4_22: And Zillah also bore Tubal-Cain, an instructor of every craftsman in bronze and iron. And the sister of Tubal-Cain was Naamah.
1_4_23: Then Lamech said to his wives: "Adah and Zillah, hear my voice; wives of Lamech, listen to my speech! For I have killed a man for wounding me, even a young man for striking me."
1_4_24: If Cain shall be avenged sevenfold, then Lamech seventy-sevenfold."
1_4_25: And Adam knew his wife again, and she bore a son and named him Seth, for she said, "God has appointed another seed for me instead of Abel, whom Cain killed."
1_4_26: And to Seth also a son was born; and he named him Enosh. Then men began to call upon the name of the LORD.
1_5_1: This is the book of the genealogy of Adam. In the day that God created man, He made him in the likeness of God.
1_5_2: He created them male and female, and blessed them and called them Mankind in the day they were created.
1_5_3: When Adam had lived one hundred and thirty years, he became the father of a son in his own likeness, according to his image, and named him Seth.
1_5_4: After he begot Seth, the days of Adam were eight hundred years; and he had sons and daughters.
1_5_5: So all the days that Adam lived were nine hundred and thirty years; and he died.
1_5_6: When Seth had lived one hundred and five years, he became the father of Enosh.
1_5_7: After he begot Enosh, Seth lived eight hundred and seven years, and had sons and daughters.
1_5_8: So all the days of Seth were nine hundred and twelve years; and he died.
1_5_9: When Enosh had lived ninety years, he became the father of Kenan.
1_5_10: After he begot Kenan, Enosh lived eight hundred and fifteen years, and had sons and daughters.
1_5_11: So all the days of Enosh were nine hundred and five years; and he died.
1_5_12: When Kenan had lived seventy years, he became the father of Mahalalel.
1_5_13: After he begot Mahalalel, Kenan lived eight hundred and forty years, and had sons and daughters.
1_5_14: So all the days of Kenan were nine hundred and ten years; and he died.
1_5_15: When Mahalalel had lived sixty-five years, he became the father of Jared.
1_5_16: After he begot Jared, Mahalalel lived eight hundred and thirty years, and had sons and daughters.
1_5_17: So all the days of Mahalalel were eight hundred and ninety-five years; and he died.
1_5_18: When Jared had lived one hundred and sixty-two years, he became the father of Enoch.
1_5_19: After he begot Enoch, Jared lived eight hundred years, and had sons and daughters.
1_5_20: So all the days of Jared were nine hundred and sixty-two years; and he died.
1_5_21: When Enoch had lived sixty-five years, he became the father of Methuselah.
1_5_22: After he begot Methuselah, Enoch walked with God three hundred years, and had sons and daughters.
1_5_23: So all the days of Enoch were three hundred and sixty-five years.
1_5_24: And Enoch walked with God; then he was not, for God took him.
1_5_25: When Methuselah had lived one hundred and eighty-seven years, he became the father of Lamech.
1_5_26: After he begot Lamech, Methuselah lived seven hundred and eighty-two years, and had sons and daughters.
1_5_27: So all the days of Methuselah were nine hundred and sixty-nine years; and he died.
1_5_28: When Lamech had lived one hundred and eighty-two years, he became the father of a son.
1_5_29: And he called his name Noah, saying, 'This one will comfort us concerning our work and the toil of our hands, because of the ground which the LORD has cursed.'
1_5_30: After he begot Noah, Lamech lived five hundred and ninety-five years, and had sons and daughters.
1_5_31: So all the days of Lamech were seven hundred and seventy-seven years; and he died.
1_5_32: Noah was five hundred years old, and Noah became the father of Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
1_6_1: Now it came to pass when men began to multiply on the face of the earth, and daughters were born to them,
1_6_2: that the sons of God saw the daughters of men were beautiful and desirable; and they took wives for themselves, whomever they chose.
1_6_3: Then the LORD said, "My Spirit shall not strive with man forever, for he is indeed flesh; yet his days shall be one hundred and twenty years."
1_6_4: The Nephilim were on the earth in those days, and also afterward, when the sons of God came in to the daughters of men and they bore children to them. Those were the mighty men who were of old, men of renown.
1_6_5: The LORD saw that the wickedness of man was great on the earth, and that every intent of the thoughts of his heart was only evil continually.
1_6_6: The LORD was sorry that He had made man on the earth, and He was grieved in His heart.
1_6_7: So the LORD said, "I will destroy man whom I have created from the face of the earth, both man and beast, creeping thing and birds of the air, for I am sorry that I have made them."
1_6_8: But Noah found favor and grace in the eyes of the LORD.
1_6_9: This is the genealogy of Noah. Noah was a righteous man, blameless in his generation, and he walked with God.
1_6_10: And Noah became the father of three sons: Shem, Ham, and Japheth.
1_6_11: The earth was corrupt before God, and the earth was filled with violence.
1_6_12: God looked upon the earth, and behold, it was corrupt; for all flesh had corrupted their way upon the earth.
1_6_13: God said to Noah, "The end of all flesh has come before Me, for the earth is filled with violence through them; and behold, I will destroy them with the earth."
1_6_14: Make yourself an ark of gopher wood; make rooms in the ark, and cover it inside and outside with pitch.
1_6_15: The length of the ark shall be three hundred cubits, its width fifty cubits, and its height thirty cubits.
1_6_16: You shall make a window for the ark to a cubit from above; and set the door of the ark in its side. You shall make it with lower, second, and third decks.
1_6_17: Behold, I Myself am bringing floodwaters on the earth to destroy all flesh in which is the breath of life; everything that is on the earth shall die.
1_6_18: But I will establish My covenant with you; and you shall go into the ark—you, your sons, your wife, and your sons' wives with you.
1_6_19: Of every living thing of all flesh you shall bring two of every sort into the ark, to keep them alive with you; they shall be male and female.
1_6_20: Of the birds after their kind, of animals after their kind, and of every creeping thing of the earth after its kind, two of every kind will come to you to keep them alive.
1_6_21: Take for yourself of all food that is eaten, and gather it to yourself; and it shall be food for you and for them."
1_6_22: Thus Noah did; according to all that God commanded him, so he did.
1_7_1: Then the LORD said to Noah, 'Come into the ark, you and all your household, for I have seen that you alone are righteous before Me in this generation.'
1_7_2: You shall take with you seven pairs of every clean animal, a male and his female, and two each of animals that are not clean, a male and his female;
1_7_3: also seven pairs of birds of the air, male and female, to keep their offspring alive on the face of all the earth.
1_7_4: For after seven more days I will cause it to rain on the earth forty days and forty nights, and I will blot out every living thing that I have made from the face of the earth.'
1_7_5: And Noah did according to all that the LORD commanded him.
1_7_6: Noah was six hundred years old when the floodwaters came upon the earth.
1_7_7: So Noah, with his sons, his wife, and his sons' wives, entered the ark because of the waters of the flood.
1_7_8: Of clean animals, of animals that are not clean, of birds, and of everything that creeps on the earth,
1_7_9: two by two they went into the ark to Noah, male and female, as God had commanded him.
1_7_10: After seven days the waters of the flood came upon the earth.
1_7_11: In the six hundredth year of Noah's life, in the second month, on the seventeenth day of the month, all the fountains of the great deep burst forth, and the windows of the heavens were opened.
1_7_12: And the rain fell upon the earth forty days and forty nights.
1_7_13: On that very same day Noah and his sons, Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and Noah's wife and the three wives of his sons entered the ark with him.
1_7_14: they and every beast after its kind, and all the cattle after their kind, every creeping thing that creeps on the earth after its kind, and every bird after its kind, every winged creature.
1_7_15: They went into the ark to Noah, two by two, of all flesh in which is the breath of life.
1_7_16: Those that entered, male and female of all flesh, went in as God had commanded him; and the LORD shut him in.
1_7_17: The flood continued forty days on the earth. The waters increased and lifted up the ark, and it rose high above the earth.
1_7_18: The waters prevailed and increased greatly on the earth, and the ark floated on the surface of the waters.
1_7_19: The waters prevailed exceedingly on the earth, and all the high mountains under the whole heaven were covered.
1_7_20: The waters prevailed fifteen cubits upward, and the mountains were covered.
1_7_21: And all flesh died that moved on the earth: birds and cattle and beasts and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth, and every man.
1_7_22: All in whose nostrils was the breath of the spirit of life, everything on the dry land, died.
1_7_23: So He destroyed all living things which were on the face of the ground: both man and cattle, creeping thing and bird of the air. They were destroyed from the earth. Only Noah and those who were with him in the ark remained alive.
1_7_24: And the waters prevailed on the earth one hundred and fifty days.
1_8_1: Then God remembered Noah and every living thing and all the animals that were with him in the ark. And God made a wind pass over the earth, and the waters subsided.
1_8_2: The fountains of the deep and the windows of heaven were closed, and the rain from the heavens was restrained.
1_8_3: And the waters receded continually from the earth, and at the end of one hundred and fifty days the waters had decreased.
1_8_4: In the seventh month, on the seventeenth day of the month, the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat.
1_8_5: The waters continued to decrease steadily until the tenth month; in the tenth month, on the first day of the month, the tops of the mountains became visible.
1_8_6: At the end of forty days, Noah opened the window of the ark that he had made.
1_8_7: Then he sent out a raven, which flew back and forth until the waters were dried up from the earth.
1_8_8: He also sent out a dove from himself to see if the waters had receded from the face of the ground.
1_8_9: But the dove found no resting place for the sole of her foot and returned to him in the ark, for the waters were on the face of the whole earth. So he put out his hand and took her, and brought her into the ark with him.
1_8_10: And he waited yet another seven days, and again he sent the dove out from the ark.
1_8_11: Then the dove came to him in the evening, and behold, a freshly plucked olive leaf was in her mouth; and Noah knew that the waters had receded from the earth.
1_8_12: So he waited yet another seven days and sent out the dove, which did not return to him anymore.
1_8_13: And it came to pass in the six hundred and first year, in the first month, on the first day of the month, that the waters were dried up from the earth; and Noah removed the covering of the ark and looked, and behold, the surface of the ground was dry.
1_8_14: And in the second month, on the twenty-seventh day of the month, the earth was completely dry.
1_8_15: Then God spoke to Noah, saying,
1_8_16: Go out of the ark, you and your wife, and your sons and your sons' wives with you.
1_8_17: Bring out with you every living thing of all flesh that is with you—birds and animals and every creeping thing that creeps on the earth—so that they may abound on the earth, and be fruitful and multiply on the earth.
1_8_18: So Noah went out, and his sons and his wife and his sons' wives with him.
1_8_19: Every animal, every creeping thing, every bird, and whatever moves on the earth, according to their families, went out of the ark.
1_8_20: Then Noah built an altar to the Lord, and took of every clean animal and of every clean bird, and offered burnt offerings on the altar.
1_8_21: And the Lord smelled the pleasing aroma. Then the Lord said in His heart, 'I will never again curse the ground because of man, for the imagination of man's heart is evil from his youth; nor will I again destroy every living thing as I have done.'
1_8_22: While the earth remains, seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, and day and night shall not cease.
1_9_1: So God blessed Noah and his sons and said to them, 'Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the earth.'
1_9_2: The fear and dread of you shall be upon every beast of the earth and upon every bird of the heavens, upon everything that creeps on the ground, and all the fish of the sea. Into your hand they are delivered.
1_9_3: Every moving thing that lives shall be food for you. Just as I gave you the green plants, I give you everything.
1_9_4: But you shall not eat flesh with its life, that is, its blood.
1_9_5: Surely for your lifeblood I will demand a reckoning; from every animal I will require it, and from man. From the hand of every man's brother I will require the life of man.
1_9_6: Whoever sheds the blood of man, by man shall his blood be shed, for in the image of God He made man.
1_9_7: And as for you, be fruitful and multiply; increase greatly on the earth and multiply in it.
1_9_8: Then God spoke to Noah and to his sons with him, saying:
1_9_9: And as for Me, behold, I establish My covenant with you and with your descendants after you,
1_9_10: and with every living creature that is with you: the birds, the livestock, and every beast of the earth with you, of all that go out of the ark, every living creature on earth.
1_9_11: I establish My covenant with you: Never again shall all flesh be cut off by the waters of the flood; never again shall there be a flood to destroy the earth.
1_9_12: And God said, 'This is the sign of the covenant which I make between Me and you and every living creature that is with you, for all future generations:'
1_9_13: I set My bow in the cloud, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between Me and the earth.
1_9_14: When I bring clouds over the earth, the bow shall be seen in the clouds,
1_9_15: and I will remember My covenant which is between Me and you and every living creature of all flesh; and the waters shall never again become a flood to destroy all flesh.
1_9_16: The rainbow shall be in the cloud, and I will look upon it to remember the everlasting covenant between God and every living creature of all flesh that is on the earth.
1_9_17: And God said to Noah, 'This is the sign of the covenant which I have established between Me and all flesh that is on the earth.'
1_9_18: The sons of Noah who went out of the ark were Shem, Ham, and Japheth. And Ham was the father of Canaan.
1_9_19: These three were the sons of Noah, and from these the whole earth was populated.
1_9_20: Noah began to be a man of the soil and planted a vineyard.
1_9_21: Then he drank of the wine and became drunk and lay uncovered in his tent.
1_9_22: And Ham, the father of Canaan, saw the nakedness of his father and told his two brothers outside.
1_9_23: But Shem and Japheth took a garment, laid it on both their shoulders, and walked backward and covered the nakedness of their father. Their faces were turned away, and they did not see their father's nakedness.
1_9_24: When Noah awoke from his wine, he knew what his youngest son had done to him.
1_9_25: Then he said, 'Cursed be Canaan; a servant of servants shall he be to his brothers.'
1_9_26: And he said, 'Blessed be the LORD, the God of Shem; and may Canaan be his servant.'
1_9_27: May God enlarge Japheth, and may he dwell in the tents of Shem; and may Canaan be his servant.
1_9_28: And Noah lived three hundred and fifty years after the flood.
1_9_29: So all the days of Noah were nine hundred and fifty years; and he died.
1_10_1: Now this is the genealogy of the sons of Noah: Shem, Ham, and Japheth. Sons were born to them after the flood.
1_10_2: The sons of Japheth were Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
1_10_3: The sons of Gomer were Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
1_10_4: The sons of Javan were Elishah, Tarshish, Kittim, and Dodanim.
1_10_5: From these the coastland peoples of the nations spread out into their lands, each with its own language, according to their families, into their nations.
1_10_6: The sons of Ham were Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.
1_10_7: The sons of Cush were Seba, Havilah, Sabtah, Raamah, and Sabteca; and the sons of Raamah were Sheba and Dedan.
1_10_8: Cush became the father of Nimrod; he began to be a mighty one on the earth.
1_10_9: He was a mighty hunter before the LORD; therefore it is said, 'Like Nimrod a mighty hunter before the LORD.'
1_10_10: And the beginning of his kingdom was Babel, Erech, Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.
1_10_11: From that land he went into Assyria and built Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir, Calah,
1_10_12: and Resen between Nineveh and Calah; that is the great city.
1_10_13: Mizraim became the father of Ludim, Anamim, Lehabim, Naphtuhim,
1_10_14: Pathrusim, Casluhim (from whom came the Philistines), and Caphtorim.
1_10_15: Canaan became the father of Sidon his firstborn, and Heth;
1_10_16: the Jebusite, the Amorite, and the Girgashite;
1_10_17: the Hivite, the Arkite, and the Sinite;
1_10_18: the Arvadite, the Zemarite, and the Hamathite. Afterward the families of the Canaanites were dispersed.
1_10_19: And the border of the Canaanites was from Sidon as you go toward Gerar, as far as Gaza; then as you go toward Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha.
1_10_20: These were the sons of Ham, according to their families, according to their languages, in their lands and in their nations.
1_10_21: And children were born also to Shem, the father of all the children of Eber, the brother of Japheth the elder.
1_10_22: The sons of Shem were Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram.
1_10_23: The sons of Aram were Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.
1_10_24: Arphaxad became the father of Shelah; and Shelah became the father of Eber.
1_10_25: To Eber were born two sons: the name of one was Peleg, for in his days the earth was divided; and his brother's name was Joktan.
1_10_26: Joktan became the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
1_10_29: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All these were the sons of Joktan.
1_10_30: And their dwelling place was from Mesha as you go toward Sephar, the hill country of the east.
1_10_31: These were the sons of Shem, according to their families, according to their languages, in their lands, according to their nations.
1_10_32: These were the families of the sons of Noah, according to their genealogies, in their nations; and from these the nations were divided on the earth after the flood.
1_11_1: Now the whole earth had one language and the same words, and all the people of the world spoke it.
1_11_2: And as they journeyed from the east, they found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there.
1_11_3: Then they said to one another, 'Come, let us make bricks and burn them thoroughly.' They had brick for stone, and they used tar for mortar.
1_11_4: And they said, 'Come, let us build ourselves a city and a tower whose top reaches to the heavens, and let us make a name for ourselves, lest we be scattered abroad over the face of the whole earth.'
1_11_5: But the LORD came down to see the city and the tower which the children of men had built.
1_11_6: And the LORD said, 'Behold, they are one people and they all have one language, and this is what they begin to do. Now nothing which they propose to do will be impossible for them.'
1_11_7: Come, let Us go down and there confuse their language, so that they will not understand one another's speech.'
1_11_8: So the LORD scattered them abroad from there over the face of all the earth, and they stopped building the city.
1_11_9: Therefore its name was called Babel, because there the LORD confused the language of all the earth; and from there the LORD scattered them abroad over the face of all the earth.
1_11_10: These are the generations of Shem. Shem was one hundred years old when he became the father of Arpachshad two years after the flood.
1_11_11: And Shem lived five hundred years after he became the father of Arpachshad, and he had other sons and daughters.
1_11_12: When Arpachshad had lived thirty-five years, he became the father of Shelah.
1_11_13: And Arpachshad lived four hundred and three years after he became the father of Shelah, and he had other sons and daughters.
1_11_14: When Shelah had lived thirty years, he became the father of Eber.
1_11_15: And Shelah lived four hundred and three years after he became the father of Eber, and he had other sons and daughters.
1_11_16: When Eber had lived thirty-four years, he became the father of Peleg.
1_11_17: And Eber lived four hundred and thirty years after he became the father of Peleg, and he had other sons and daughters.
1_11_18: When Peleg had lived thirty years, he became the father of Reu.
1_11_19: And Peleg lived two hundred and nine years after he became the father of Reu, and he had other sons and daughters.
1_11_20: When Reu had lived thirty-two years, he became the father of Serug.
1_11_21: And Reu lived two hundred and seven years after he became the father of Serug, and he had other sons and daughters.
1_11_22: When Serug had lived thirty years, he became the father of Nahor.
1_11_23: And Serug lived two hundred years after he became the father of Nahor, and he had other sons and daughters.
1_11_24: When Nahor had lived twenty-nine years, he became the father of Terah.
1_11_25: And Nahor lived one hundred and nineteen years after he became the father of Terah, and he had other sons and daughters.
1_11_26: When Terah had lived seventy years, he became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
1_11_27: Now these are the generations of Terah. Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran; and Haran became the father of Lot.
1_11_28: And Haran died before his father Terah in the land of his birth, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
1_11_29: And Abram and Nahor took wives: the name of Abram's wife was Sarai, and the name of Nahor's wife was Milcah, the daughter of Haran, the father of Milcah and Iscah.
1_11_30: But Sarai was barren; she had no child.
1_11_31: And Terah took his son Abram and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran, and his daughter-in-law Sarai, his son Abram's wife, and they went out together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go to the land of Canaan; but when they came to Haran, they settled there.
1_11_32: So the days of Terah were two hundred and five years, and Terah died in Haran.
1_12_1: Now the LORD said to Abram, "Get out of your country, from your kindred and your father's house, to a land that I will show you."
1_12_2: I will make you a great nation; I will bless you and make your name great, and you shall be a blessing."
1_12_3: I will bless those who bless you, and I will curse him who curses you; and in you all the families of the earth shall be blessed."
1_12_4: So Abram departed as the LORD had spoken to him, and Lot went with him. Abram was seventy-five years old when he departed from Haran.
1_12_5: Abram took Sarai his wife and Lot his brother's son, and all their possessions that they had accumulated, and the people whom they had acquired in Haran, and they set out for the land of Canaan. When they came to the land of Canaan,
1_12_6: Abram passed through the land to the place of Shechem, to the oak of Moreh. At that time the Canaanites were in the land.
1_12_7: Then the LORD appeared to Abram and said, "To your descendants I will give this land." So he built an altar there to the LORD, who had appeared to him.
1_12_8: From there he moved on to the hill country on the east of Bethel, and pitched his tent with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east. There he built an altar to the LORD and called on the name of the LORD.
1_12_9: Then Abram journeyed on, continuing toward the Negev.
1_12_10: Now there was a famine in the land, and Abram went down to Egypt to dwell there, for the famine was severe in the land.
1_12_11: When he was about to enter Egypt, he said to Sarai his wife, "Indeed I know that you are a woman of beautiful countenance."
1_12_12: When the Egyptians see you, they will say, 'This is his wife.' Then they will kill me, but they will let you live."
1_12_13: Please say you are my sister, that it may go well with me because of you, and that my life may be spared on your account."
1_12_14: When Abram entered Egypt, the Egyptians saw that the woman was very beautiful.
1_12_15: The princes of Pharaoh also saw her and praised her to Pharaoh, and the woman was taken into Pharaoh's household.
1_12_16: He treated Abram well for her sake. Abram acquired sheep, oxen, male and female donkeys, male and female servants, and camels.
1_12_17: But the LORD plagued Pharaoh and his household with great plagues because of Sarai, Abram's wife.
1_12_18: And Pharaoh called Abram and said, "What is this you have done to me? Why did you not tell me that she was your wife?"
1_12_19: Why did you say, 'She is my sister,' so that I took her as my wife? Now therefore, here is your wife; take her and go your way."
1_12_20: So Pharaoh commanded his men concerning him; and they sent him away, with his wife and all that he had.
1_13_1: So Abram went up from Egypt, he and his wife and all that he had, and Lot with him, into the Negev.
1_13_2: Now Abram was very rich in livestock, in silver, and in gold.
1_13_3: He went on his journeys from the Negev as far as Bethel, to the place where his tent had been at the beginning, between Bethel and Ai,
1_13_4: to the place where he had made an altar at the first; and there Abram called on the name of the LORD.
1_13_5: Now Lot, who went with Abram, also had flocks and herds and tents.
1_13_6: But the land could not support them while dwelling together, for their possessions were so great that they could not remain together.
1_13_7: And there was strife between the herdsmen of Abram's livestock and the herdsmen of Lot's livestock. At that time the Canaanites and the Perizzites were dwelling in the land.
1_13_8: So Abram said to Lot, "Please let there be no strife between you and me, and between my herdsmen and your herdsmen; for we are relatives."
1_13_9: Is not the whole land before you? Please separate from me. If you take the left, then I will go to the right; or if you go to the right, then I will go to the left."
1_13_10: And Lot lifted up his eyes and saw all the plain of the Jordan, that it was well watered everywhere like the garden of the LORD, like the land of Egypt, in the direction of Zoar. (This was before the LORD destroyed Sodom and Gomorrah.)
1_13_11: So Lot chose for himself all the plain of the Jordan, and Lot journeyed eastward. Thus they separated from each other.
1_13_12: Abram settled in the land of Canaan, while Lot settled in the cities of the plain and moved his tents as far as Sodom.
1_13_13: Now the men of Sodom were exceedingly wicked and sinners against the LORD.
1_13_14: The LORD said to Abram, after Lot had separated from him, "Lift up your eyes and look from the place where you are—northward and southward and eastward and westward;"
1_13_15: for all the land that you see I will give to you and to your descendants forever.
1_13_16: I will make your descendants as the dust of the earth, so that if anyone can count the dust of the earth, then your descendants can also be numbered.
1_13_17: Arise, walk through the land in its length and breadth; for I will give it to you."
1_13_18: Then Abram moved his tent and came and settled by the oaks of Mamre, which are in Hebron, and there he built an altar to the LORD.
1_14_1: In those days, King Amraphel of Shinar, King Arioch of Ellasar, King Chedorlaomer of Elam, and King Tidal of Goiim came together in the region.
1_14_2: These kings waged war against King Bera of Sodom, King Birsha of Gomorrah, King Shinab of Admah, King Shemeber of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar).
1_14_3: All these came as allies and joined forces in the Valley of Siddim (that is, the Salt Sea, or Dead Sea).
1_14_4: Twelve years they had been subject to Chedorlaomer, but in the thirteenth year they rebelled against him.
1_14_5: In the fourteenth year Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him came and defeated the Rephaim in Ashteroth-karnaim, the Zuzim in Ham, and the Emim in Shaveh-kiriathaim,
1_14_6: and the Horites in their hill country of Mount Seir, as far as El-paran, which is by the edge of the wilderness.
1_14_7: Then they turned back and came to En-mishpat (that is, Kadesh), and conquered all the territory of the Amalekites, as well as the Amorites who lived in Hazazon-tamar.
1_14_8: Then the king of Sodom, the king of Gomorrah, the king of Admah, the king of Zeboiim, and the king of Bela (that is, Zoar) went out and lined up for battle in the Valley of Siddim,
1_14_9: against King Chedorlaomer of Elam, King Tidal of Goiim, King Amraphel of Shinar, and King Arioch of Ellasar—four kings against five.
1_14_10: Now the Valley of Siddim was full of tar pits; and as the kings of Sodom and Gomorrah fled, some fell into them, but the rest escaped to the hill country.
1_14_11: The four kings took all the goods of Sodom and Gomorrah and all their food supply, and went their way.
1_14_12: They also took Lot, Abram's nephew who dwelt in Sodom, along with his possessions, and departed.
1_14_13: Then one who had escaped came and reported to Abram the Hebrew, who was living by the oak grove belonging to Mamre the Amorite, brother of Eshcol and brother of Aner. These were allies of Abram.
1_14_14: When Abram heard that his nephew had been taken captive, he assembled his 318 trained men, born in his household, and pursued the captors as far as Dan.
1_14_15: He divided his forces against them by night, he and his servants, and defeated them, pursuing them as far as Hobah, north of Damascus.
1_14_16: He brought back all the goods and also brought back his nephew Lot with his possessions, as well as the women and the other people.
1_14_17: After Abram's return from the defeat of Chedorlaomer and the kings who were with him, the king of Sodom came out to meet him in the Valley of Shaveh (that is, the King's Valley).
1_14_18: Then Melchizedek, king of Salem, brought out bread and wine; he was a priest of God Most High.
1_14_19: He blessed him and said, 'Blessed be Abram by God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth;
1_14_20: and blessed be God Most High, who has delivered your enemies into your hand.' Then Abram gave him a tenth of all the goods he had recovered.
1_14_21: The king of Sodom said to Abram, 'Give me the people, but take the goods for yourself.'
1_14_22: But Abram said to the king of Sodom, 'I have raised my hand in an oath to the LORD, God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth,
1_14_23: that I will not take anything that belongs to you, from a thread to a sandal strap, so you can never say, "I have made Abram rich."
1_14_24: I will take nothing except what the young men have eaten, and the portion of the men who went with me—Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre; let them take their share.'
1_15_1: After these things the word of the LORD came to Abram in a vision, saying, 'Do not be afraid, Abram. I am your shield, your exceedingly great reward.'
1_15_2: But Abram said, 'Lord GOD, what will You give me, seeing I go childless, and the heir of my house is Eliezer of Damascus?'
1_15_3: Then Abram said, 'Look, You have given me no offspring; indeed one born in my house is my heir!'
1_15_4: And behold, the word of the LORD came to him, saying, 'This one shall not be your heir, but one who will come from your own body shall be your heir.'
1_15_5: Then He brought him outside and said, 'Look now toward heaven, and count the stars if you are able to number them.' And He said to him, 'So shall your descendants be.'
1_15_6: And he believed in the LORD, and He counted it to him as righteousness.
1_15_7: Then He said to him, 'I am the LORD who brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans, to give you this land to inherit it.'
1_15_8: And he said, 'Lord GOD, how shall I know that I will inherit it?'
1_15_9: So He said to him, 'Bring Me a three-year-old heifer, a three-year-old female goat, a three-year-old ram, a turtledove, and a young pigeon.'
1_15_10: Then he brought all these to Him and cut them in two, down the middle, and laid each half opposite the other; but he did not cut the birds in two.
1_15_11: And when the birds of prey came down on the carcasses, Abram drove them away.
1_15_12: Now when the sun was going down, a deep sleep fell upon Abram; and behold, a dreadful and great darkness fell upon him.
1_15_13: Then He said to Abram: 'Know certainly that your descendants will be strangers in a land that is not theirs, and will serve them, and they will afflict them four hundred years.'
1_15_14: And also the nation whom they serve I will judge; afterward they shall come out with great possessions.
1_15_15: Now as for you, you shall go to your fathers in peace; you shall be buried at a good old age.
1_15_16: But in the fourth generation they shall return here, for the iniquity of the Amorites is not yet complete.'
1_15_17: And it came to pass, when the sun went down and it was dark, that behold, there appeared a smoking oven and a burning torch that passed between those pieces.
1_15_18: On the same day the LORD made a covenant with Abram, saying: 'To your descendants I have given this land, from the river of Egypt to the great river, the River Euphrates—'
1_15_19: the Kenites, the Kenezzites, the Kadmonites,
1_15_20: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim,
1_15_21: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.'
1_16_1: Now Sarai, Abram's wife, had borne him no children. She had an Egyptian servant whose name was Hagar.
1_16_2: So Sarai said to Abram, "Since the LORD has restrained me from bearing children, please go in to my maid; perhaps I shall obtain children by her." And Abram listened to the voice of Sarai.
1_16_3: After Abram had lived ten years in the land of Canaan, Sarai, Abram's wife, took Hagar the Egyptian, her servant, and gave her to Abram her husband as a wife.
1_16_4: He went in to Hagar, and she conceived. When she saw that she had conceived, her mistress was despised in her eyes.
1_16_5: Then Sarai said to Abram, "May the wrong done to me be upon you! I gave my maid into your embrace, and when she saw that she had conceived, I was despised in her sight. May the LORD judge between you and me."
1_16_6: But Abram said to Sarai, "Behold, your maid is in your hand; do to her as you please." So Sarai dealt harshly with her, and she fled from her presence.
1_16_7: Now the Angel of the LORD found her by a spring of water in the wilderness, by the spring on the way to Shur.
1_16_8: And He said, "Hagar, Sarai's maid, where have you come from and where are you going?" She said, "I am fleeing from the presence of my mistress Sarai."
1_16_9: The Angel of the LORD said to her, "Return to your mistress and submit yourself under her authority."
1_16_10: Then the Angel of the LORD said to her, "I will greatly multiply your descendants so that they shall not be numbered for multitude."
1_16_11: And the Angel of the LORD said to her: "Behold, you are with child and shall bear a son. You shall call his name Ishmael, because the LORD has heard your affliction."
1_16_12: He shall be a wild donkey of a man; his hand shall be against everyone, and everyone's hand against him. And he shall dwell in the presence of all his relatives."
1_16_13: Then she called the name of the LORD who spoke to her, "You are the God who sees me," for she said, "Have I truly seen the One who sees me here?"
1_16_14: Therefore the well was called Beer-lahai-roi; behold, it is between Kadesh and Bered.
1_16_15: So Hagar bore Abram a son, and Abram named his son, whom Hagar bore, Ishmael.
1_16_16: Abram was eighty-six years old when Hagar bore Ishmael to him.
1_17_1: When Abram was ninety-nine years old, the LORD appeared to Abram and said to him, "I am God Almighty; walk before Me and be blameless."
1_17_2: And I will establish My covenant between Me and you, and will multiply you exceedingly."
1_17_3: Then Abram fell on his face, and God talked with him, saying:
1_17_4: "As for Me, behold, My covenant is with you, and you shall be the father of a multitude of nations."
1_17_5: No longer shall your name be called Abram, but your name shall be Abraham; for I have made you the father of a multitude of nations."
1_17_6: I will make you exceedingly fruitful, and I will make nations of you, and kings shall come from you."
1_17_7: And I will establish My covenant between Me and you and your descendants after you throughout their generations for an everlasting covenant, to be God to you and to your descendants after you."
1_17_8: Also I give to you and your descendants after you the land in which you are a stranger, all the land of Canaan, as an everlasting possession; and I will be their God."
1_17_9: And God said to Abraham: "As for you, you shall keep My covenant, you and your descendants after you throughout their generations."
1_17_10: This is My covenant which you shall keep, between Me and you and your descendants after you: Every male child among you shall be circumcised;"
1_17_11: and you shall be circumcised in the flesh of your foreskins, and it shall be a sign of the covenant between Me and you."
1_17_12: He who is eight days old among you shall be circumcised, every male child in your generations, he who is born in your house or bought with money from any foreigner who is not your descendant."
1_17_13: He who is born in your house and he who is bought with your money must be circumcised, and My covenant shall be in your flesh for an everlasting covenant."
1_17_14: And the uncircumcised male child, who is not circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin, that person shall be cut off from his people; he has broken My covenant."
1_17_15: Then God said to Abraham, "As for Sarai your wife, you shall not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall be her name."
1_17_16: And I will bless her and also give you a son by her; then I will bless her, and she shall become nations; kings of peoples shall be from her."
1_17_17: Then Abraham fell on his face and laughed, and said in his heart, "Shall a child be born to a man who is one hundred years old? And shall Sarah, who is ninety years old, bear a child?"
1_17_18: And Abraham said to God, "Oh, that Ishmael might live before You!"
1_17_19: Then God said: "No, Sarah your wife shall bear you a son, and you shall call his name Isaac; I will establish My covenant with him for an everlasting covenant, and with his descendants after him."
1_17_20: And as for Ishmael, I have heard you. Behold, I have blessed him, and will make him fruitful, and will multiply him exceedingly. He shall become the father of twelve princes, and I will make him a great nation."
1_17_21: But My covenant I will establish with Isaac, whom Sarah shall bear to you at this set time next year."
1_17_22: Then He finished talking with him, and God went up from Abraham."
1_17_23: So Abraham took Ishmael his son, all who were born in his house and all who were bought with his money, every male among the men of Abraham's house, and circumcised the flesh of their foreskins that very same day, as God had said to him."
1_17_24: Abraham was ninety-nine years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin."
1_17_25: And Ishmael his son was thirteen years old when he was circumcised in the flesh of his foreskin."
1_17_26: That very same day Abraham was circumcised, and his son Ishmael;"
1_17_27: and all the men of his house, born in the house or bought with money from a foreigner, were circumcised with him."
1_18_1: Then the LORD appeared to Abraham by the oak trees of Mamre while he was sitting at the entrance of his tent during the heat of the day.
1_18_2: He lifted up his eyes and looked, and behold, three men were standing near him. When he saw them, he ran from the tent entrance to meet them and bowed himself to the ground.
1_18_3: And said, 'My lord, if I have found favor in your sight, please do not pass by your servant.'
1_18_4: Please let a little water be brought, that you may wash your feet and rest yourselves under the tree.
1_18_5: And I will bring a piece of bread, that you may refresh yourselves; after that you may pass by, since you have come to your servant.' They said, 'Do as you have said.'
1_18_6: So Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah and said, 'Quickly, prepare three measures of fine flour, knead it, and make bread.'
1_18_7: And Abraham ran to the herd and took a tender and choice calf and gave it to the servant, who hastened to prepare it.
1_18_8: Then he took curds and milk and the calf which he had prepared, and set it before them; and he stood by them under the tree as they ate.
1_18_9: Then they said to him, 'Where is Sarah your wife?' And he said, 'There, in the tent.'
1_18_10: And He said, 'I will surely return to you at this time next year, and behold, Sarah your wife will have a son.' And Sarah was listening at the tent entrance behind him.
1_18_11: Now Abraham and Sarah were old, well advanced in age; and Sarah had passed the age of childbearing.
1_18_12: Therefore Sarah laughed within herself, saying, 'After I have grown old, shall I have pleasure, my lord being old also?'
1_18_13: And the LORD said to Abraham, 'Why did Sarah laugh, saying, "Shall I indeed bear a child, when I am so old?"'
1_18_14: Is anything too hard for the LORD? At the appointed time I will return to you, and Sarah shall have a son.'
1_18_15: But Sarah denied it, saying, 'I did not laugh,' for she was afraid. And He said, 'No, but you did laugh.'
1_18_16: Then the men rose from there and looked toward Sodom, and Abraham went with them to send them on the way.
1_18_17: And the LORD said, 'Shall I hide from Abraham what I am about to do,
1_18_18: since Abraham will surely become a great and mighty nation, and all the nations of the earth shall be blessed through him?'
1_18_19: For I have chosen him, so that he will command his children and his household after him to keep the way of the LORD by doing what is right and just, that the LORD may bring to Abraham all that He has spoken to him.'
1_18_20: And the LORD said, 'Because the outcry against Sodom and Gomorrah is great, and because their sin is very grave,
1_18_21: I will go down now and see whether they have done entirely according to the outcry that has come to Me; and if not, I will know.'
1_18_22: Then the men turned away from there and went toward Sodom, but Abraham remained standing before the LORD.
1_18_23: And Abraham drew near and said, 'Will You really sweep away the righteous with the wicked?'
1_18_24: Suppose there are fifty righteous within the city; would You also destroy the place and not spare it for the sake of the fifty righteous who are in it?'
1_18_25: Far be it from You to do such a thing, to slay the righteous with the wicked, so that the righteous should be as the wicked. Far be it from You! Shall not the Judge of all the earth do right?'
1_18_26: So the LORD said, 'If I find fifty righteous within the city of Sodom, I will spare all the place for their sake.'
1_18_27: Then Abraham answered and said, 'Indeed now, I who am but dust and ashes have taken it upon myself to speak to the Lord:
1_18_28: Suppose there were five less than the fifty righteous; would You destroy all of the city for lack of five?' So He said, 'If I find there forty-five, I will not destroy it.'
1_18_29: And he spoke to Him yet again and said, 'Suppose there should be forty found there?' So He said, 'I will not do it for the sake of forty.'
1_18_30: Then he said, 'Let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak: Suppose thirty should be found there?' So He said, 'I will not do it if I find thirty there.'
1_18_31: And he said, 'Indeed now, I have taken it upon myself to speak to the Lord: Suppose twenty should be found there?' So He said, 'I will not destroy it for the sake of twenty.'
1_18_32: Then he said, 'Let not the Lord be angry, and I will speak but once more: Suppose ten should be found there?' And He said, 'I will not destroy it for the sake of ten.'
1_18_33: So the LORD went His way when He had finished speaking with Abraham; and Abraham returned to his place.
1_19_1: The two angels came to Sodom in the evening, and Lot was sitting in the gate of Sodom. When Lot saw them, he rose to meet them and bowed himself with his face toward the ground.
1_19_2: He said, 'Please, my lords, turn aside into your servant's house and spend the night, and wash your feet; then you may rise early and go on your way.' But they said, 'No, we will spend the night in the open square.'
1_19_3: Yet he urged them strongly, so they turned aside to him and entered his house. He prepared a feast for them, baking unleavened bread, and they ate.
1_19_4: Before they lay down, the men of the city, the men of Sodom, both young and old, all the people from every quarter, surrounded the house.
1_19_5: They called out to Lot and said to him, 'Where are the men who came to you tonight? Bring them out to us that we may have relations with them.'
1_19_6: So Lot went out to them through the doorway and shut the door behind him,
1_19_7: and said, 'Please, my brothers, do not do such a wicked thing!
1_19_8: See now, I have two daughters who have not known a man; please, let me bring them out to you, and you may do to them as you wish; only do nothing to these men, since they have come under the shelter of my roof.'
1_19_9: But they said, 'Stand back!' And they said, 'This one came in as an outsider and he keeps acting as a judge; now we will deal worse with you than with them.' So they pressed hard against the man Lot, and came near to break down the door.
1_19_10: But the men reached out their hands and pulled Lot into the house with them, and shut the door.
1_19_11: They struck the men who were at the doorway of the house with blindness, both small and great, so that they wearied themselves trying to find the door.
1_19_12: Then the men said to Lot, 'Have you anyone else here—a son-in-law, your sons, your daughters, or anyone else in the city? Take them out of this place,
1_19_13: for we are about to destroy this place, because the outcry against them has grown so great before the LORD that the LORD has sent us to destroy it.'
1_19_14: So Lot went out and spoke to his sons-in-law, who were to marry his daughters, and said, 'Get up, get out of this place; for the LORD will destroy this city!' But he seemed to his sons-in-law to be joking.
1_19_15: When the morning dawned, the angels urged Lot to hurry, saying, 'Arise, take your wife and your two daughters who are here, lest you be swept away in the punishment of the city.'
1_19_16: But he hesitated. So the men seized his hand and the hand of his wife and the hands of his two daughters, the LORD being merciful to him, and they brought him out and set him outside the city.
1_19_17: When they had brought them outside, one said, 'Escape for your life! Do not look behind you, and do not stay anywhere in the plain; escape to the mountains, lest you be destroyed.'
1_19_18: Then Lot said to them, 'Please, no, my lords!
1_19_19: Behold, your servant has found favor in your sight, and you have shown me great kindness in saving my life; but I cannot escape to the mountains, for the disaster will overtake me and I will die.
1_19_20: See now, this city is near enough to flee to, and it is a little one. Please let me escape there—is it not a little one?—that my life may be saved.'
1_19_21: And he said to him, 'Behold, I grant you this request also, not to overthrow the city of which you have spoken.
1_19_22: Hurry, escape there, for I cannot do anything until you arrive there.' Therefore the name of the city was called Zoar.
1_19_23: The sun had risen over the earth when Lot came to Zoar.
1_19_24: Then the LORD rained on Sodom and Gomorrah brimstone and fire from the LORD out of heaven,
1_19_25: and He overthrew those cities, all the plain, all the inhabitants of the cities, and what grew on the ground.
1_19_26: But his wife looked back from behind him, and she became a pillar of salt.
1_19_27: And Abraham went early in the morning to the place where he had stood before the LORD.
1_19_28: And he looked down toward Sodom and Gomorrah, and toward all the land of the plain; and he saw, and behold, the smoke of the land ascended like the smoke of a furnace.
1_19_29: When God destroyed the cities of the plain, God remembered Abraham, and sent Lot out of the midst of the overthrow, when He overthrew the cities in which Lot had dwelt.
1_19_30: Then Lot went up out of Zoar and stayed in the mountains with his two daughters; for he was afraid to dwell in Zoar. And he dwelt in a cave, he and his two daughters.
1_19_31: Now the firstborn said to the younger, 'Our father is old, and there is not a man on earth to come in to us as is the custom of all the earth.
1_19_32: Come, let us make our father drink wine, and we will lie with him, that we may preserve offspring through our father.'
1_19_33: So they made their father drink wine that night. And the firstborn went in and lay with her father, and he did not know when she lay down or when she arose.
1_19_34: On the following day, the firstborn said to the younger, 'Behold, I lay last night with my father; let us make him drink wine tonight also; then you go in and lie with him, that we may preserve offspring through our father.'
1_19_35: So they made their father drink wine that night also. And the younger arose and lay with him, and he did not know when she lay down or when she arose.
1_19_36: Thus both the daughters of Lot were with child by their father.
1_19_37: The firstborn bore a son and called his name Moab; he is the father of the Moabites to this day.
1_19_38: And the younger, she also bore a son and called his name Ben-Ammi; he is the father of the Ammonites to this day.
1_20_1: Abraham journeyed from there toward the Negev and settled between Kadesh and Shur, then sojourned in Gerar as a foreigner.
1_20_2: Abraham said of Sarah his wife, 'She is my sister.' So King Abimelech of Gerar sent and took Sarah to his palace.
1_20_3: But God came to Abimelech in a dream by night and said to him, 'You are a dead man because of the woman whom you have taken, for she is a married woman.'
1_20_4: Now Abimelech had not come near her; and he said, 'Lord, will you destroy an innocent nation?'
1_20_5: Did you not say to me, 'She is my sister'? And she herself said, 'He is my brother.' I did this with a clear conscience and innocent hands.'
1_20_6: Then God said to him in the dream, 'Yes, I know that you did this in the integrity of your heart, and it was I who kept you from sinning against me; therefore I did not let you touch her.'
1_20_7: Now return the man's wife, for he is a prophet, and he will pray for you and you will live. But if you do not restore her, know that you shall surely die, you and all who are yours.'
1_20_8: So Abimelech rose early in the morning and called all his servants together and told them all these things; and the men were greatly afraid.
1_20_9: Then Abimelech called Abraham and said to him, 'What have you done to us? How have I offended you, that you have brought on me and my kingdom a great sin? You have done deeds to me that ought not to be done.'
1_20_10: Abimelech said to Abraham, 'What were you thinking, that you did this thing?'
1_20_11: Abraham said, 'Because I thought, surely there is no fear of God in this place, and they will kill me because of my wife.'
1_20_12: But indeed she is truly my sister. She is the daughter of my father, but not the daughter of my mother; and she became my wife.
1_20_13: And when God caused me to wander from my father's house, I said to her, 'This is the kindness you shall show to me: at every place to which we go, say of me, He is my brother.'"
1_20_14: Then Abimelech took sheep and oxen and male and female servants, and gave them to Abraham; and he restored Sarah his wife to him.
1_20_15: And Abimelech said, 'Behold, my land is before you; dwell where it pleases you.'
1_20_16: And to Sarah he said, 'I have given your brother a thousand pieces of silver; it is your vindication before all who are with you, and you are completely vindicated.'
1_20_17: So Abraham prayed to God, and God healed Abimelech and his wife and his female servants, so that they bore children;
1_20_18: for the LORD had closed up all the wombs of the house of Abimelech because of Sarah, Abraham's wife.
1_21_1: And the LORD visited Sarah as He had said, and the LORD did for Sarah as He had promised, keeping His word and fulfilling everything He had spoken.
1_21_2: For Sarah conceived and bore Abraham a son in his old age, at the appointed time of which God had spoken to him, at just the time God had said it would happen.
1_21_3: And Abraham called the name of his son who was born to him—whom Sarah bore to him—Isaac.
1_21_4: Then Abraham circumcised his son Isaac when he was eight days old, as God had commanded him.
1_21_5: Now Abraham was one hundred years old when his son Isaac was born to him.
1_21_6: And Sarah said, 'God has made me laugh, and all who hear will laugh with me.'
1_21_7: She also said, 'Who would have said to Abraham that Sarah would nurse children? Yet I have borne him a son in his old age.'
1_21_8: So the child grew and was weaned. And Abraham made a great feast on the day that Isaac was weaned.
1_21_9: And Sarah saw the son of Hagar the Egyptian, whom she had borne to Abraham, mocking.
1_21_10: Therefore she said to Abraham, 'Cast out this bondwoman and her son; for the son of this bondwoman shall not be heir with my son, namely with Isaac.'
1_21_11: And the matter was very displeasing in Abraham's sight because of his son.
1_21_12: But God said to Abraham, 'Do not let it be displeasing in your sight because of the lad or because of your bondwoman. Whatever Sarah has said to you, listen to her voice; for in Isaac your seed shall be called.'
1_21_13: Yet I will also make a nation of the son of the bondwoman, because he is your seed.'
1_21_14: So Abraham rose early in the morning, and took bread and a skin of water; and putting it on her shoulder, he gave it and the boy to Hagar, and sent her away. Then she departed and wandered in the Wilderness of Beersheba.
1_21_15: And the water in the skin was used up, and she placed the boy under one of the shrubs.
1_21_16: Then she went and sat down opposite him at a distance of about a bowshot; for she said, 'Let me not see the death of the boy.' So she sat opposite him, and lifted her voice and wept.
1_21_17: And God heard the voice of the lad. Then the angel of God called to Hagar out of heaven, and said to her, 'What ails you, Hagar? Fear not, for God has heard the voice of the lad where he is.'
1_21_18: Arise, lift up the lad and hold him with your hand, for I will make him a great nation.'
1_21_19: Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well of water. And she went and filled the skin with water, and gave the lad a drink.
1_21_20: So God was with the lad; and he grew and dwelt in the wilderness, and became an archer.
1_21_21: He dwelt in the Wilderness of Paran; and his mother took a wife for him from the land of Egypt.
1_21_22: And it came to pass at that time that Abimelech and Phicol, the commander of his army, spoke to Abraham, saying, 'God is with you in all that you do.'
1_21_23: Now therefore, swear to me by God that you will not deal falsely with me, with my offspring, or with my posterity; but that according to the kindness that I have done to you, you will do to me and to the land in which you have dwelt.'
1_21_25: Then Abraham rebuked Abimelech because of a well of water which Abimelech's servants had seized.
1_21_26: And Abimelech said, 'I do not know who has done this thing; you did not tell me, nor had I heard of it until today.'
1_21_27: So Abraham took sheep and oxen and gave them to Abimelech, and the two of them made a covenant.
1_21_28: And Abraham set seven ewe lambs of the flock by themselves.
1_21_29: Then Abimelech asked Abraham, 'What is the meaning of these seven ewe lambs which you have set by themselves?'
1_21_30: And he said, 'You will take these seven ewe lambs from my hand, that they may be my witness that I have dug this well.'
1_21_31: Therefore he called that place Beersheba, because the two of them swore an oath there.
1_21_32: Thus they made a covenant at Beersheba. So Abimelech rose with Phicol, the commander of his army, and they returned to the land of the Philistines.
1_21_33: Then Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beersheba, and there called on the name of the LORD, the Everlasting God.
1_21_34: And Abraham stayed in the land of the Philistines many days.
1_22_1: After these things God tested Abraham and said to him, "Abraham!" And he said, "Here I am."
1_22_2: Then He said, "Take now your son, your only son Isaac, whom you love, and go to the land of Moriah, and offer him there as a burnt offering on one of the mountains of which I shall tell you."
1_22_3: So Abraham rose early in the morning and saddled his donkey, and took two of his young men with him, and Isaac his son; and he split the wood for the burnt offering, and arose and went to the place of which God had told him.
1_22_4: On the third day Abraham lifted his eyes and saw the place from a distance.
1_22_5: And Abraham said to his young men, "Stay here with the donkey; the boy and I will go over there to worship, and we will come back to you."
1_22_6: So Abraham took the wood of the burnt offering and laid it on Isaac his son; and he took in his hand the fire and the knife, and the two of them went together.
1_22_7: But Isaac spoke to Abraham his father and said, "My father!" And he said, "Here I am, my son." Then he said, "Look, the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for a burnt offering?"
1_22_8: And Abraham said, "My son, God will provide for Himself the lamb for the burnt offering." So the two of them went together.
1_22_9: Then they came to the place of which God had told him. And Abraham built an altar there and laid the wood in order; and he bound Isaac his son and laid him on the altar, upon the wood.
1_22_10: And Abraham stretched out his hand and took the knife to slay his son.
1_22_11: But the Angel of the LORD called to him from heaven and said, "Abraham, Abraham!" So he said, "Here I am."
1_22_12: And He said, "Do not lay your hand on the lad, or do anything to him; for now I know that you fear God, since you have not withheld your son, your only son, from Me."
1_22_13: Then Abraham lifted his eyes and looked, and behold, behind him was a ram caught in a thicket by its horns. So Abraham went and took the ram, and offered it up for a burnt offering instead of his son.
1_22_14: And Abraham called the name of that place The-LORD-Will-Provide; as it is said to this day, "On the mount of the LORD it shall be provided."
1_22_15: Then the Angel of the LORD called to Abraham a second time out of heaven,
1_22_16: and said: "By Myself I have sworn, says the LORD, because you have done this thing, and have not withheld your son, your only son—
1_22_17: blessing I will bless you, and multiplying I will multiply your descendants as the stars of the heaven and as the sand which is on the seashore; and your descendants shall possess the gate of their enemies."
1_22_18: In your seed all the nations of the earth shall be blessed, because you have obeyed My voice."
1_22_19: So Abraham returned to his young men, and they rose and went together to Beersheba; and Abraham dwelt at Beersheba.
1_22_20: Now it came to pass after these things that it was told Abraham, saying, "Indeed Milcah also has borne children to your brother Nahor:"
1_22_21: Uz his firstborn, Buz his brother, Kemuel the father of Aram,
1_22_22: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel."
1_22_23: And Bethuel begot Rebekah. These eight Milcah bore to Nahor, Abraham's brother.
1_22_24: His concubine, whose name was Reumah, also bore Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
1_23_1: Sarah lived one hundred and twenty-seven years; these were the years of the life of Sarah.
1_23_2: So Sarah died in Kiriath-arba (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan, and Abraham came to mourn for Sarah and to weep for her.
1_23_3: Then Abraham stood up from before his dead and spoke to the sons of Heth, saying,
1_23_4: I am a foreigner and a sojourner among you. Give me property for a burial place among you that I may bury my dead out of my sight.
1_23_5: And the sons of Heth answered Abraham, saying to him,
1_23_6: Hear us, my lord: you are a mighty prince among us; bury your dead in the choicest of our burial places. None of us will withhold from you his grave, that you may bury your dead.
1_23_7: Then Abraham stood up and bowed himself to the people of the land, the sons of Heth.
1_23_8: And he spoke with them, saying, If it is your wish that I bury my dead out of my sight, hear me, and meet with Ephron the son of Zohar for me,
1_23_9: that he may give me the cave of Machpelah which he owns, which is at the end of his field. For the full price let him give it to me in your presence for a burial place.
1_23_10: Now Ephron was sitting among the sons of Heth; and Ephron the Hittite answered Abraham in the hearing of the sons of Heth, even of all who entered at the gate of his city, saying,
1_23_11: No, my lord, hear me: I give you the field and the cave that is in it; I give it to you in the presence of the sons of my people. I give it to you. Bury your dead!
1_23_12: Then Abraham bowed himself down before the people of the land;
1_23_13: and he spoke to Ephron in the hearing of the people of the land, saying, If you will give it, please hear me. I will give you money for the field; take it from me and I will bury my dead there.
1_23_14: And Ephron answered Abraham, saying to him,
1_23_15: My lord, listen to me; the land is worth four hundred shekels of silver. What is that between you and me? So bury your dead.
1_23_16: And Abraham listened to Ephron; and Abraham weighed out for Ephron the silver which he had named in the hearing of the sons of Heth, four hundred shekels of silver, currency of the merchants.
1_23_17: So the field of Ephron which was in Machpelah, which was before Mamre, the field and the cave which was in it, and all the trees that were in the field, which were within all the surrounding borders, were deeded
1_23_18: to Abraham as a possession in the presence of the sons of Heth, before all who went in at the gate of his city.
1_23_19: And after this, Abraham buried Sarah his wife in the cave of the field of Machpelah, before Mamre (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan.
1_23_20: So the field and the cave that is in it were deeded to Abraham by the sons of Heth as property for a burial place.
1_24_1: Now Abraham was old, well advanced in age; and the LORD had blessed Abraham in all things.
1_24_2: Abraham said to his servant, the oldest of his household who had charge of all that he owned, 'Please, put your hand under my thigh,
1_24_3: and I will make you swear by the LORD, the God of heaven and the God of earth, that you will not take a wife for my son from the daughters of the Canaanites, among whom I live;
1_24_4: but you shall go to my country and to my family, and take a wife for my son Isaac.'
1_24_5: The servant said to him, 'Perhaps the woman will not be willing to follow me to this land. Must I take your son back to the land from which you came?'
1_24_6: Abraham said to him, 'See to it that you do not take my son back there.
1_24_7: The LORD, the God of heaven, who took me from my father's house and from the land of my family, and who spoke to me and swore to me, saying, 'To your descendants I will give this land,' He will send His angel before you, and you shall take a wife for my son from there.
1_24_8: But if the woman is not willing to follow you, then you will be released from this oath; only do not take my son back there.'
1_24_9: So the servant put his hand under the thigh of Abraham his master, and swore to him concerning this matter.
1_24_10: Then the servant took ten of his master's camels and departed, taking all kinds of choice gifts from his master; and he went to Mesopotamia, to the city of Nahor.
1_24_11: He made the camels kneel down outside the city by the well of water at evening time, the time when women go out to draw water.
1_24_12: Then he said, 'O LORD, God of my master Abraham, please give me success this day, and show kindness to my master Abraham.
1_24_13: I am standing here by the well of water, and the daughters of the men of the city are coming out to draw water.
1_24_14: Now let it be that the young woman to whom I say, 'Please let down your jar that I may drink,' and who says, 'Drink, and I will also give your camels a drink'—let her be the one You have appointed for Your servant Isaac. And by this I will know that You have shown kindness to my master.'
1_24_15: Before he had finished speaking, behold, Rebekah, who was born to Bethuel, son of Milcah, the wife of Abraham's brother Nahor, came out with her water jar on her shoulder.
1_24_16: Now the young woman was very beautiful and fair to look upon, a virgin; no man had known her. And she went down to the well, filled her jar, and came up.
1_24_17: Then the servant ran to meet her and said, 'Please give me a little water to drink from your jar.'
1_24_18: She said, 'Drink, my lord.' And she quickly lowered her jar to her hand, and gave him a drink.
1_24_19: When she had finished giving him a drink, she said, 'I will also draw water for your camels until they have finished drinking.'
